scenario: Sable Carrier handles 5000 shipments per week through Camunda 8.8 Forms. A customs consent checkbox on the export form must enforce that the operator actively confirms the declaration before submission, even on the 800 last-minute shipments per day that go through the same task within 10 minutes of cutoff.
question: Which checkbox property under Validation enforces that the form only submits when the box has a value?

options.text: The Required validation property, which holds the form submission until the checkbox contains a value and otherwise shows a validation error on the field
The Read only property turned on, which blocks the operator from changing the box and so guarantees that the original value is the one that gets submitted
The Disabled property turned on, which removes the box from interaction at runtime and forces the operator to use a separate confirmation step instead
The Hide if expression returning false, which keeps the checkbox visible at all times and is documented as the proper way to enforce mandatory consent

a.text: Correct. Under Validation, the Required property states that the checkbox must contain a value, and the form will only submit when that condition is fulfilled, otherwise a validation error is displayed.

b.text: Incorrect. Read only prevents the operator from changing the value, but it does not enforce that the box was actively set before submission.

c.text: Incorrect. Disabled is documented for use during development; it does not enforce that the operator has consented at runtime.

d.text: Incorrect. Hide if controls visibility through an expression and does not enforce that the operator has set the box before submitting the form.

explanation: The Required validation property is the documented way to enforce that a checkbox must contain a value before submission, with a validation error shown otherwise.
